name: Cranberry Coupe Melba
recipeIngredient: 6 Scoops vanilla ice cream, about 1 1/2 pints
recipeInstructions:
1. Blanch the peaches in boiling water for 30 seconds. Immediately rinse with cold running water; drain well. Peel the peaches and cut into halves, removing and discarding the pits.
2. Heat 2 cups of water, sugar, and vanilla in a medium saucepan until boiling. Boil, stirring occasionally, for 5 minutes until the sugar is completely dissolved. Add peach halves and poach until tender but still firm, about 10 minutes. Remove peaches with a slotted spoon to a bowl and refrigerate until cold, about 1 hour.
3. Add cranberries to the syrup. Heat to boiling and reduce heat. Simmer for 10 minutes. Puree the mixture in a food processor or blender and stir in kirsch. Refrigerate, covered, until cold, about 1 hour.
4. At serving time, stand 2 peach halves upright in each of 6 stemmed glasses. Place a scoop of ice cream between the halves and spoon cranberry puree over the ice cream.
Notes:
If fresh peaches aren't available, you can use canned freestone (Elberta) peaches. The cranberry puree has a nubby texture; if you want a smoother sauce, press the puree through a fine-mesh sieve to remove bits of skin.
